title: When The Suspect Is Smarter Than The Detective

beats.description: Cold open snippet of suspect refusing to sign documents or confess, establishing immediate tension.
Narrator introduces the 2011 7-Eleven robbery, police response, and the arrest of a known heroin addict experiencing withdrawal.
Explains the footage's origin (suspect's own YouTube channel), legal stakes in Georgia, and frames the interrogation as a 'master class' in self-defense.
Round 1 interrogation: Miranda rights read, suspect asserts silence, detective attempts pressure, narrator explains optimal legal responses.
Two-hour time jump as suspect is returned to the interrogation room for the next session.
Round 2 interrogation: Suspect requests methadone for withdrawal, denies crime, narrator details the Reid technique and its psychological manipulation.
Suspect explicitly invokes right to counsel, detective escalates to false evidence claims and parole bargaining, suspect cites discovery rules to shut down questioning.
Suspect experiences medical distress, is released after 12 hours, turns his life around, and continues educating viewers via his channel.
